nodes.name: Every Monday 07:00
nodes.notes: Fires every Monday at 07:00 server time. Adjust cron expression if needed: '0 7 * * 1' = Monday 7am.

nodes.name: Fetch Reddit (AI Topics)
nodes.notes: Fetches top weekly posts from relevant subreddits. No auth required for public subreddits.

nodes.name: Fetch RSS Feed
nodes.notes: Fetches from RSS_FEED_1 variable. Default: Hacker News front page. Set RSS_FEED_1 in n8n Variables.

parameters.jsonBody:
={
  "model": "mistral",
  "prompt": "You are a research assistant for a solo creative technologist. From the following list of articles and posts collected this week, write a 200-word intelligence brief covering: (1) the 2-3 most important trends, (2) anything directly actionable for someone running a self-hosted AI infrastructure, and (3) one thing to watch over the next 2 weeks.

Sources:
{{ $json.reddit_titles }}

Brief format: '## This Week in Tech

**Key Trends:**
...

**Actionable for your stack:**
...

**Watch:**
...'",
  "stream": false
}

nodes.name: AI: Generate Brief
nodes.notes: Uses local Ollama to summarise collected sources into a weekly intelligence brief. Mistral works well for this task.

nodes.name: Send to Telegram
nodes.notes: Sends the completed brief to your Telegram. You receive it every Monday morning as a personal briefing.

meta.description: Pulls weekly content from Reddit (AI subreddits) and RSS feeds every Monday morning. Summarises them into an intelligence brief using local Ollama and delivers it to Telegram. A lightweight alternative to expensive newsletter curation services.
meta.chapter_reference: Part III, Chapter 14 — n8n and the Automation Layer
customise_suggestions: Change subreddits in the Reddit URL to match your niche
Set RSS_FEED_1 variable to your preferred RSS source
Adjust the AI prompt to focus on your specific topics
Add a Code node to also save the brief to a MinIO file for archiving
